name: SurrealDB (Docker)
description: Connect to a SurrealDB instance or Surreal Cloud via Docker. Run SurrealQL queries, manage records, inspect namespaces and databases, and explore schemas through the official SurrealDB MCP server in an isolated container.

inputs.label: SurrealDB Endpoint URL
inputs.description: SurrealDB connection endpoint. Use host.docker.internal instead of localhost to reach a SurrealDB instance running on the host from inside Docker (e.g., ws://host.docker.internal:8000/rpc).

inputs.label: Namespace
inputs.description: The SurrealDB namespace to connect to.

inputs.label: Database
inputs.description: The SurrealDB database to connect to.

inputs.label: Username
inputs.description: The SurrealDB username to authenticate with.

inputs.label: Password
inputs.description: The SurrealDB password to authenticate with.

inputs.label: Surreal Cloud Access Token
inputs.description: Surreal Cloud access token, used instead of username/password to connect to a Surreal Cloud instance. Leave empty for a self-hosted SurrealDB instance.

obtain.instructions:
1. Sign in to Surreal Cloud
2. Open your instance settings
3. Generate or copy an access token
4. Paste it here

auth.instructions: Provide endpoint, namespace, database and username/password for a self-hosted SurrealDB instance, or a Surreal Cloud access token for Surreal Cloud. All connection values are optional and can also be supplied per-session through the MCP connection tools.
